name: scroll position is preserved across the editor<->preview toggle
comment: Toggling between the rendered preview and the plain-text editor must land you at the SAME relative position, not jump to the top. Sync = the outgoing view's scroll fraction is applied to the incoming one (proportional; the two views are never on screen together). AutoPilot's AX layer cannot read a WKWebView's rendered scroll offset, so the app is launched with --expose-scroll-fraction, which surfaces the editor's and preview's fraction (0.000-1.000) as AX-readable labels editorScrollFractionLabel / previewScrollFractionLabel. This plan: open a long .md (auto-preview ON), scroll the preview to the bottom, read its fraction, toggle to the editor, assert the editor fraction matches, toggle back, assert the preview fraction matches. NEGATIVE CONTROL: at the very start (never scrolled) the preview fraction must be ~0 — so a later 'matches' assertion can't be a false pass from everything sitting at the top. Requires staged /tmp/medit-ap-long.md and the Debug build launched with --expose-scroll-fraction.

steps.comment: Confirm the test-hook labels exist; if not, the flag didn't take and the rest is meaningless.
NEGATIVE CONTROL: a freshly opened doc's preview is at the top. Establishes that a later non-zero fraction is a real scroll, not a default.
Scroll the preview well down. deltaY large enough to reach the bottom of a 242-line doc.
Preview is no longer at the top — proves the scroll registered before we test that the toggle preserves it.
The core assertion: after toggling off the preview, the editor is scrolled to roughly where the preview was — NOT reset to the top. A regression (no sync) would show 0.000 here.
Round-trip: toggling back to the preview restores its scrolled position too, not the top.
